evidence: get_running_config (R2) - interface Serial0/1/0: "ip access-group R2_IN in"
get_running_config (R2) - access-list R2_IN entries: sequence 10 deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.5; sequence 20 deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.6
get_ospf (R2) - Serial0/1/0: "interface_state: Point-to-Point ... neighbors: []" (no OSPF neighbors seen on R2)
get_ospf (R1) - Serial0/1/0: neighbor 2.2.2.2 at 10.0.0.2 (state ospf-nbr-full) — R1 receives R2's OSPF packets while R2 does not see R1

plan_history.intent_description: OSPF adjacency on R2 is down because inbound ACL R2_IN on Serial0/1/0 is denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6). Restore adjacency by allowing OSPF hellos to be received on R2.
plan_history.problem: Inbound access-list R2_IN applied to interface Serial0/1/0 on R2 contains deny entries for destination 224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6, blocking OSPF hellos and preventing OSPF adjacency with R1.
plan_history.fix_summary: Remove the deny entries for 224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6 from access-list R2_IN (applied inbound on Serial0/1/0) so R2 can receive OSPF multicast packets and form adjacency with R1.

parameters: remove access-list R2_IN sequence 10: deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.5
remove access-list R2_IN sequence 20: deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.6

remaining_issues: Access-list R2_IN still exists on R2 and contains deny entries for destination 224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6 (from running-config). If R2_IN is reapplied inbound anywhere in future, OSPF multicast could be blocked again; consider removing or updating these deny ACEs or documenting intended use of R2_IN.
